note: Reverses the fix for CVE-2025-53942 (authentik OAuth/SAML deactivated-user bypass). The user-login stage already checked `if not user.is_active` and logged a warning ('User is not active, login will not work.'), but before the fix it did NOT stop the login; the fix added `return self.executor.stage_invalid()`. This suite deletes that return, leaving a guard that warns but doesn't enforce, so deactivated users complete login and can authorize applications. The GTIG 'hardcoded trust assumption' class in its purest form: the check looks present (and even logs that login won't work) but is a no-op.

bugs.owasp: A07: Identification and Authentication Failures (CWE-285 Improper Authorization / deactivated account)
bugs.severity_note: GHSA-9g4j-v8w5-7x42 (CVE-2025-53942) is a half-authenticated bypass: deactivated accounts can authorize OAuth/SAML apps but cannot reach the API. Mapped to the board's medium, not inflated; a reviewer rating it high is not penalized harshly given the auth impact.
bugs.description: Reverts the fix for CVE-2025-53942. In the user-login stage's do_login, the code reads the pending user and checks `if not user.is_active`, logging a warning that 'login will not work.' The fix made that check enforce by adding `return self.executor.stage_invalid()`; the regression deletes that return. The result is a guard that detects the inactive account and even logs that login should fail, but then falls through and logs the user in anyway, so a deactivated user (e.g. one enrolled or connected via an OAuth/SAML source) reaches a half-authenticated state and can authorize applications. The reviewer must notice that the `if not user.is_active` branch no longer returns / blocks, so the warning is toothless and the deactivated-account check does nothing. Reading the line ('there is an is_active check, looks fine') is not enough; the catch is recognizing the check no longer enforces. Flagging only that 'the warning is misleading' without the auth-bypass impact is a partial catch.

injection.replace_with:
if not user.is_active:
            self.logger.warning("User is not active, login will not work.")
